_comment: Binds the committed SP1 guest ELF to the verifying keys it must be deployed with. verify-vkey-pin.sh asserts sha256(program/elf/teth-pool-prover) == elf_sha256 (always) and, where the SP1 toolchain is present, that the vkey derived from the ELF == program_vkey. A guest rebuild that changes the ELF MUST regenerate the vkey and update this file IN THE SAME COMMIT, then redeploy SP1PoolRootVerifier with the new PROGRAM_VKEY — otherwise every proveStateTransition reverts and all withdrawals brick.
guest_state: MAINNET-READY — denom canonical/pairwise-distinct asserts + CXFER checked_add; incremental-state public-values tail (frontiers + null/UTXO entries) for cross-cycle persistence; 0x2A T_WITHDRAW + 0x60-0x64 bridge ops dispatched from the Taproot reveal witness only (the dead bare-OP_RETURN bridge dispatch was removed — dapp emits Taproot exclusively, and mainnet relay requires it since bridge envelopes 164-537B exceed the 80B OP_RETURN cap); BIP141 64-byte-tx reject + coinbase invariant. Cargo unit tests cover Taproot-envelope extraction + structural pins on dispatch placement + null_set consumption. Rebuilt on the vast prover host (non-Docker); canonical-elf.yml CI rebuilds in the official SP1 Docker image and asserts byte-equality — if CI fails the pin must be regenerated from the Docker artifact before deploy.
